task.title: `--add-noqa` for diagnostics on blank lines results in bad whitespace
task.description:
Repository: astral-sh/ruff
Issue #23751

### Summary

Rules like `D100` can produce diagnostics on `TextRange::ZERO` even if the line is blank.  In these cases, running ruff with `--add-noqa` produces a line with leading whitespace:

Create an empty file, and run `ruff check --add-noqa`:

```
$ echo "" > test.py

$ ruff check --isolated --select D100 --add-noqa test.py
Added 1 noqa directive.

$ cat test.py
  # noqa: D100
```

### Version

main

rating.rationale: The agent produced no output whatsoever — no code changes, no communication, no HANDOFF.md, nothing. This is the worst possible outcome: a task was assigned and the agent either never started or silently failed without any indication. Complete recovery is needed with a new agent assignment.
